title: JsonEnvelope
description:
The one common envelope every verb emits. Never prompts; prose (TTY) is rendered from the
SAME typed value where practical.

next_actions.description: Stable, machine-actionable next steps — each carries a complete argv.

receipt.description: The durable idempotency record — present on **every** terminal op, including failures.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

ActionCode.title: ActionCode
ActionCode.description: A machine-actionable next-action code. The values in `examples` are the known set; an unrecognized future code is still valid and MUST be executed via the action's `argv` (never rejected).

Affected.description: What an outcome refers to (every field optional — a policy flip has no skill/version).

version.description: A `version_id` (commit SHA-256, lowercase hex) when the outcome refers to a version.

NextAction.description:
A machine-actionable next step. The `argv` is the ready-to-exec command; `code` lets an agent
branch on the known set and still pass through unknowns. The three safety fields are ADDITIVE
and optional (absent = unknown) — a producer fills them from its one rules module, never
per call site, so the classification cannot drift between surfaces.

argv.description: A complete argv array — execute as-is (no TTY parsing).

mutates.description:
Whether executing `argv` CHANGES state — local files (skill bytes, the sidecar) or shared
workspace state. Absent = unknown (e.g. a bare `RETRY`, whose safety is the retried
command's own).

needs_network.description: Whether executing `argv` dials the plane. Absent = unknown.

risk_note.description:
A one-line caution for an action whose effect a human/agent should weigh before running
(team-visible moves, local discards). Absent = nothing to flag.

Receipt.description:
The ONE canonical receipt across all outcomes — the durable idempotency record (present even
on failures): one stable receipt per op, identical on retry.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp (the plane stamps it; never an ambient clock in `topos-core`).

op_id.description: Client-minted UUIDv4, persisted before the first send.

schema_version.description: Always `1` for this contract version (the schema pins it `const`).

TerminalOutcome.description: The closed set of terminal outcomes the agent branches on. SCREAMING_SNAKE on the wire.

oneOf.description:
A successfully-opened proposal — an explicit `--propose`, or a direct publish/revert the
per-bundle protection DOWNGRADED to one (the receipt's `details.downgraded` says which).

WireError.description:
The flat wire error (rich `thiserror` enums stay internal). Carries a
stable code + retryability + the safe next actions; never raw SQL/git strings.

code.description:
A stable, machine-branchable error code, distinct from (and finer than) `outcome`. This is an
**open, additive** string vocabulary — it is intentionally NOT a closed enum: new codes are
added over time, and a consumer must treat an unrecognized `code` as the `outcome` it carries.

context.description: Structured context (no TTY prose to parse).
